basic_details.job_title: Union Bank of India SO Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 395 Officer & Specialist Officer Posts
basic_details.post_name: General Banking Segment (Post Codes G01–G19) and Specialized Segment (Post Codes S01–S108)
basic_details.short_information: Union Bank of India has released the Union Bank Recruitment Project 2026–27 (Officers with Domain Expertise, General Banking Officers & Specialist Officers) notification for a total of 395 vacancies spanning General Banking Officers and Specialist Officers, from Manager (MMGS-II) up to Deputy General Manager (TEGS-VI). Online registration opens on 21 July 2026 and closes on 10 August 2026. This article covers important dates, vacancy break-up, eligibility, age limit, salary, application fee, selection process, and the step-by-step application procedure, based strictly on the official notificat

important_dates.cut-off date for age eligibility: 01.07.2026 (1st day of the month registration commences)

application_fee.details: Fee can be paid online via Debit Card (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards/Mobile Wallets/UPI. Bank transaction charges are borne by the candidate. Fees, once paid, are non-refundable and cannot be held in reserve for future examinations.

age_limit.details: Age limits vary post-wise as detailed in the tables above. The cut-off date for age eligibility is 01.07.2026 (the first day of the month in which online registration commences). | Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC is cumulative with only one of the remaining categories (PwBD/Ex-servicemen/1984 riot-affected). There is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in the Officers' Cadre vacancies.

salary_details.details: In addition to basic pay, Special Allowance, Dearness Allowance, and other allowances are payable per the Bank's rules. Officers are also eligible for residential quarters/lease rent, LFC, medical/hospitalization reimbursement, and other perquisites. Salary and emoluments are governed by the industry-level bipartite settlement and Joint Note applicable to bank officers. CTC may vary based on location, DA changes, wage revision, and Bank schemes.

selection_process: Test No. of Questions Maximum Marks Duration
Professional Knowledge relevant to the post 50 (MCQ) 100 60 minutes
Professional Knowledge relevant to the post/domain 50 100
For posts up to Scale III
depending on the number of applicants. For posts in Scale IV
selection may comprise Group Discussion (if required)
and/or Personal Interview. The Bank reserves the right to add
or modify the selection method for any notified post.

and English Language sections are qualifying in nature - marks in these are not counted toward final merit. All tests except English Language are available bilingually (English and Hindi). Minimum qualifying marks: 40% for General/EWS categories
subject to the Bank's discretion. There is a penalty of 25% of the marks assigned to a question for each wrong answer; unattempted questions carry no penalty.
A Personal Interview of 100 marks assesses academic and job knowledge
and suitability for the post. Minimum qualifying marks: 50 for General/EWS
45 for Reserved Category (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD). Candidates are called for interview in the ratio of 1:3 against the number of vacancies (subject to change at the Bank's discretion).
Final selection is based on the aggregate marks of Online Examination and Personal Interview (or Personal Interview alone
or GD/other assessment plus Personal Interview
depending on which stages are conducted)
up to the number of vacancies. In case of a tie
the candidate senior in age (earlier date of birth) is ranked higher.
how_to_apply: Visit the official website www.unionbankofindia.co.in and click on the 'Recruitment' page.

On the Career Overview page, click 'Click to View the Current Recruitment' to open the link "UNION BANK RECRUITMENT PROJECT 2026-27 (OFFICERS WITH DOMAIN EXPERTISE, GENERAL BANKING OFFICERS & SPECIALIST OFFICERS)".
Click on "CLICK HERE TO APPLY ONLINE" to open the online application form.
Choose "Click here for New Registration" and enter your Name, contact details, and Email ID. Note down the system-generated Provisional Registration Number and Password.
Use the "SAVE AND NEXT" option to fill the application in stages if needed, and verify all details carefully - no changes are allowed after clicking "COMPLETE REGISTRATION".
Upload photograph, signature, and left thumb impression as per the prescribed specifications.
Fill in the remaining application details, preview the form, and click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION".
Click on the "Payment" tab and pay the applicable application fee online.
Click 'Submit' and save/print the system-generated application form and e-receipt for future reference.

Candidates can apply for only one post. Only the last valid, complete application will be retained in case of multiple submissions. An application, once submitted, cannot be withdrawn, and the fee is non-refundable.
important_instructions: Application can be made for only one post; multiple appearances for a single post at interview will lead to cancellation of candidature. No change of category will be permitted at any stage after registration of the online application. Existing Union Bank of India employees may apply through proper channel (HR Department of Regional/Zonal Office) and, if selected, must resign and rejoin the Bank's service afresh. Selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years of active service. Selected candidates for posts G01–G19 must execute a Service Indemnity Bond to serve the Bank for a minimum of 3 years, or pay ₹2,00,000 plus applicable taxes if they leave before completing 3 years. Selected candidates for posts S01–S108 must execute a Service Indemnity Bond to serve the Bank for a minimum of 3 years, or pay ₹2,50,000 plus applicable taxes if they leave before completing 3 years. Selected candidates shall be posted at any Branch/Office of the Bank throughout India at the Bank's sole discretion. The Bank reserves the right to change/modify the number of vacancies, examination structure, or cancel the recruitment process at any stage. Any dispute arising out of the notification is subject to the sole jurisdiction of courts in Mumbai.

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The 395 vacancies are distributed across five scale/grade levels as follows: The 395 vacancies are further divided into 108 individual post codes (G01–G19 under General Banking, S01–S108 under the Specialized Segment covering IT, Forex, Chartered Accountancy, Risk Management, Law, Architecture, Engineering, Fire Safety, Printing, and Vendor Management roles). Post-wise vacancy numbers, scale, age limit, qualification, and experience for all 108 posts are provided in the Eligibility section below.

tables.caption: Category-wise Reservation Break-up (Total across all 108 posts)

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Eligibility Criteria (Post-wise)
content_sections.text: An applicant must be: (i) a citizen of India, or (ii) a subject of Nepal, or (iii) a subject of Bhutan, or (iv) a Tibetan refugee who migrated to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India, or (v) a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, Kenya, Uganda, Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia, or Vietnam with the intention of permanently settling in India. Candidates under categories (ii)–(v) must hold a certificate of eligibility issued by the Government of India before the date of this notification. The Specialized Segment covers Forex, IT (API Management, Cybersecurity, Cloud, DevSecOps, Data Science, Network Administration, Full Stack/Flutter/MERN Development, Firewall/WAF/PAM/DAM Administration, and more), Chartered Accountants (Taxation, Balance Sheet, Capital Planning, Investor Relations, IND-AS, MFTP, Credit), Risk Management Officers, Law Officers, Architects, Engineers (Civil, Electrical, Fire), a Printing Specialist, and IT Vendor Management Professionals. Because this segment spans 90 individual post codes with distinct qualifications and experience requirements, the complete post-wise table is presented below. Vacancy counts, category-wise break-up (SC/ST/OBC/EWS/UR), and PwBD reservation for every individual post code (G01–G19, S01–S108) are listed in the official notification's reservation tables and can be checked by post code against the total figures given above.

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Age Limit
content_sections.text: Age limits vary post-wise as detailed in the tables above. The cut-off date for age eligibility is 01.07.2026 (the first day of the month in which online registration commences). Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC is cumulative with only one of the remaining categories (PwBD/Ex-servicemen/1984 riot-affected). There is no reservation for Ex-servicemen in the Officers' Cadre vacancies.

rows.category: Ex-Servicemen / Commissioned Officers (incl. ECOs/SSCOs, min. 5 years military service)

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Salary
content_sections.text: In addition to basic pay, Special Allowance, Dearness Allowance, and other allowances are payable per the Bank's rules. Officers are also eligible for residential quarters/lease rent, LFC, medical/hospitalization reimbursement, and other perquisites. Salary and emoluments are governed by the industry-level bipartite settlement and Joint Note applicable to bank officers. CTC may vary based on location, DA changes, wage revision, and Bank schemes.

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fee can be paid online via Debit Card (RuPay/Visa/MasterCard/Maestro), Credit Card, Internet Banking, IMPS, Cash Cards/Mobile Wallets/UPI. Bank transaction charges are borne by the candidate. Fees, once paid, are non-refundable and cannot be held in reserve for future examinations.

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Selection Process
content_sections.text: For posts up to Scale III, selection may comprise Online Examination, Group Discussion (if required), and/or Personal Interview, depending on the number of applicants. For posts in Scale IV, V, and VI, selection may comprise Group Discussion (if required), any other test/assessment, and/or Personal Interview. The Bank reserves the right to add, cancel, or modify the selection method for any notified post. Quantitative Aptitude, Reasoning, and English Language sections are qualifying in nature - marks in these are not counted toward final merit. All tests except English Language are available bilingually (English and Hindi). Minimum qualifying marks: 40% for General/EWS categories, 35% for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD), subject to the Bank's discretion. There is a penalty of 25% of the marks assigned to a question for each wrong answer; unattempted questions carry no penalty. A Personal Interview of 100 marks assesses academic and job knowledge, power of expression, clarity of thought, leadership qualities, communication skills, and suitability for the post. Minimum qualifying marks: 50 for General/EWS, 45 for Reserved Category (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD). Candidates are called for interview in the ratio of 1:3 against the number of vacancies (subject to change at the Bank's discretion). Final selection is based on the aggregate marks of Online Examination and Personal Interview (or Personal Interview alone, or GD/other assessment plus Personal Interview, depending on which stages are conducted), prepared in descending order category-wise, up to the number of vacancies. In case of a tie, the candidate senior in age (earlier date of birth) is ranked higher.

rows.test: Professional Knowledge relevant to the post

tables.caption: Online Examination Structure - General Banking Officers (Post Codes G01–G19, up to Scale III)

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Documents Required
content_sections.text: Printout of the valid Interview Call Letter System-generated printout of the online application form Proof of Date of Birth (Birth Certificate or SSLC/Std. X Certificate with DOB) Photo Identity Proof (PAN Card/Passport/Permanent Driving Licence/Voter's Card/Bank Passbook with photo/Aadhaar, etc. - Ration Card and Learner's Driving Licence are NOT accepted) Mark sheets and certificates/degree for educational qualifications Latest Caste Certificate (for SC/ST/OBC candidates, in prescribed Government format) OBC Non-Creamy Layer certificate issued on or after 01.04.2026 (for OBC candidates) Income & Asset Certificate based on FY 2025-26, valid for FY 2026-27 (for EWS candidates) Disability certificate in prescribed format (for PwBD candidates) Scribe declaration form, if services of a scribe were used Discharge book/pension payment order (for Ex-servicemen) No Objection Certificate from employer (for candidates serving in Government/PSU/Bank) Experience certificate in the prescribed format (Annexure-II of the notification), issued by the Competent Authority of the employer, clearly stating period, post held, and nature of duties Appointment letters, salary certificates, and pay slips are not accepted as proof of work experience.

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: How to Apply
content_sections.text: Visit the official website www.unionbankofindia.co.in and click on the 'Recruitment' page. On the Career Overview page, click 'Click to View the Current Recruitment' to open the link "UNION BANK RECRUITMENT PROJECT 2026-27 (OFFICERS WITH DOMAIN EXPERTISE, GENERAL BANKING OFFICERS & SPECIALIST OFFICERS)". Click on "CLICK HERE TO APPLY ONLINE" to open the online application form. Choose "Click here for New Registration" and enter your Name, contact details, and Email ID. Note down the system-generated Provisional Registration Number and Password. Use the "SAVE AND NEXT" option to fill the application in stages if needed, and verify all details carefully - no changes are allowed after clicking "COMPLETE REGISTRATION". Upload photograph, signature, and left thumb impression as per the prescribed specifications. Fill in the remaining application details, preview the form, and click "COMPLETE REGISTRATION". Click on the "Payment" tab and pay the applicable application fee online. Click 'Submit' and save/print the system-generated application form and e-receipt for future reference. Candidates can apply for only one post. Only the last valid, complete application will be retained in case of multiple submissions. An application, once submitted, cannot be withdrawn, and the fee is non-refundable.

content_sections.heading: Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Application can be made for only one post; multiple appearances for a single post at interview will lead to cancellation of candidature. No change of category will be permitted at any stage after registration of the online application. Existing Union Bank of India employees may apply through proper channel (HR Department of Regional/Zonal Office) and, if selected, must resign and rejoin the Bank's service afresh. Selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years of active service. Selected candidates for posts G01–G19 must execute a Service Indemnity Bond to serve the Bank for a minimum of 3 years, or pay ₹2,00,000 plus applicable taxes if they leave before completing 3 years. Selected candidates for posts S01–S108 must execute a Service Indemnity Bond to serve the Bank for a minimum of 3 years, or pay ₹2,50,000 plus applicable taxes if they leave before completing 3 years. Selected candidates shall be posted at any Branch/Office of the Bank throughout India at the Bank's sole discretion. The Bank reserves the right to change/modify the number of vacancies, examination structure, or cancel the recruitment process at any stage. Any dispute arising out of the notification is subject to the sole jurisdiction of courts in Mumbai.

content_sections.text: Apply Online: Click here Official Notification PDF : Click here Official Website: Click here Join Arattai Channel: Click Here Join Telegram Channel: Click Here Join WhatsApp Channel : Click Here Sarkari Result : Click Here Download Mobile App: Click Here Candidates who have completed their graduate or postgraduate degree can also explore more Graduate Government Jobs and Bank Jobs in India matching their qualification. faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online registration and fee payment window closes on 10 August 2026 at 23:59:59 Hrs.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 395 total vacancies across General Banking Officer posts (G01–G19) and Specialist Officer posts (S01–S108).

faq.question: Q3. What is the application fee for Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: ₹177 (inclusive of GST) for SC/ST/PwBD candidates and ₹1,180 (inclusive of GST) for UR/EWS/OBC candidates.

faq.question: Q4. What is the selection process for Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: For posts up to Scale III, selection may involve an Online Examination and/or Group Discussion and/or Personal Interview. For Scale IV, V, and VI posts, selection may involve Group Discussion/any other assessment and/or Personal Interview, depending on the number of applicants.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Age limits vary by post and scale, ranging from a minimum of 22 years to a maximum of 50 years depending on the specific post code. The age cut-off date is 1 July 2026.

faq.question: Q6. Is there a written exam for Union Bank of India Specialist Officer posts?
faq.answer: Yes - Specialist Officer posts (S01–S108, up to Scale III) have a Professional Knowledge test of 50 questions/100 marks in 60 minutes, followed by a Personal Interview.

faq.question: Q7. Can existing Union Bank of India employees apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Yes, subject to fulfilling eligibility criteria, existing employees may apply through proper channel via their Regional/Zonal Office HR Department, and must resign and rejoin the Bank's service afresh if selected.

faq.question: Q8. What is the probation period for selected candidates?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years of active service from the date of joining.

faq.question: Q9. Is there a service bond for Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates selected for posts G01–G19 must serve a minimum of 3 years or pay ₹2,00,000 plus taxes; candidates selected for posts S01–S108 must serve a minimum of 3 years or pay ₹2,50,000 plus taxes, if they leave early.

faq.question: Q10. Where will Union Bank of India Recruitment 2026 exams be held?
faq.answer: The Bank has listed tentative examination centers across 30 states/UTs, including cities such as Mumbai, Delhi/NCR, Chennai, Bengaluru, Hyderabad, Kolkata, and others. The Bank reserves the right to change the allotted centre.
